name: EPS Austria Online Bank Transfer API
what_it_does: EPS (Electronic Payment Standard) Austria online bank transfer API for Austrian merchants to accept guaranteed bank transfers from 95%+ of Austrian bank customers through Austria's national online banking payment standard, operated by a consortium of Austrian banks including Erste Bank, Raiffeisen, and UniCredit Austria. Enables AI agents to manage EPS payment initiation for Austrian e-commerce checkout automation, handle payment confirmation for order fulfillment automation, access transaction status for verification automation, retrieve payment result for checkout completion automation, manage redirect flow for Austrian banking authentication automation, handle refund processing for payment reversal automation, and integrate EPS with Austrian e-commerce, utilities, government services, and subscription platforms for end-to-end Austrian bank transfer automation.
use_cases: Accepting guaranteed bank transfers from Austrian consumers for e-commerce checkout automation agents
Processing Austrian utility and government service payments via EPS bank transfer agents
Integrating EPS as preferred local payment method for Austrian market e-commerce automation agents
Handling Austrian subscription and recurring payment confirmation for SaaS billing automation agents
not_for: Non-Austrian markets (EPS is Austria-specific payment standard)
International IBAN wire transfers across multiple EU countries (use SEPA direct debit)
Card payments or digital wallets (EPS is online bank transfer only)
best_when: An Austrian merchant or European e-commerce platform targeting Austrian consumers wanting AI agents to accept guaranteed bank transfers through EPS, Austria's dominant online banking payment method covering 95%+ of Austrian bank customers.
avoid_when: ACCESSED VIA PSP ONLY: EPS is not directly accessible — merchants must integrate through a certified PSP (Payment Service Provider) such as Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, or WiPay; automated direct EPS API assumption creates no direct access; automated must use PSP that supports EPS. REDIRECT TO ONLINE BANKING REQUIRED: EPS requires consumer to authenticate in their bank's online banking portal; automated background payment processing creates integration failure; automated checkout must redirect consumer to bank authentication. SYNCHRONOUS CONFIRMATION NOT GUARANTEED: EPS payment confirmation can arrive seconds to minutes after bank authentication; automated immediate synchronous confirmation assumption creates premature order fulfillment; automated must handle asynchronous payment notification. AUSTRIA-ONLY COVERAGE: EPS consumers must hold an account at an Austrian bank participating in the EPS network; automated non-Austrian consumer payment via EPS creates unsupported_bank error; automated must show EPS only to Austrian consumers.

auth.notes: EPS is accessed via PSP (Payment Service Provider) APIs — not directly. PSP handles EPS integration with their own auth. Vienna, Austria. Operated by Austrian bank consortium (Erste, Raiffeisen, UniCredit Austria, BAWAG). Founded 2002. Products: Online bank transfer, payment confirmation, redirect flow. SDKs: Via PSPs (Stripe, Adyen, etc.). FMA (Austrian Financial Market Authority) regulated. Covers 95%+ of Austrian online banking customers. 50+ participating banks. Austria's most used online bank transfer method.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Testing via PSP sandbox; production requires PSP merchant account
pricing.paid_tiers: Via PSP pricing: typically 0.3-1.5% per transaction depending on PSP; no direct EPS fees to merchant
pricing.notes: Vienna AT. Bank consortium. FMA regulated. Fees via PSP. Austria-only coverage.

performance.rate_limits: No direct rate limits (PSP-mediated); bank redirect latency varies; webhook-based payment confirmation

known_agent_gotchas: PSP REQUIRED — NO DIRECT API: EPS does not offer a direct merchant API; automated direct integration creates no access point; automated must use a certified PSP (Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Hobex, etc.) that supports EPS as a payment method

CONSUMER BANK REDIRECT IS MANDATORY: EPS requires consumer to authenticate in their bank's online banking interface; automated server-side payment without consumer redirect creates payment initiation failure; automated checkout must redirect consumer to EPS redirect URL

ASYNC PAYMENT CONFIRMATION: EPS payment result may arrive asynchronously after bank authentication; automated synchronous confirmation polling creates timeout errors; automated must implement webhook/IPN handler for EPS payment confirmation callback

AUSTRIA-ONLY BANK COVERAGE: EPS is only available to consumers with Austrian participating bank accounts; automated offering EPS to non-Austrian consumers creates payment_method_unavailable; automated must geo-filter or check consumer banking country before showing EPS

PSP-SPECIFIC IMPLEMENTATION: EPS implementation details vary by PSP (Stripe vs Adyen vs PayPal vs local Austrian PSPs); automated PSP-agnostic EPS assumption creates PSP-specific errors; automated must follow target PSP's EPS integration guide specifically
agent_readiness.security_notes: Austrian national bank transfer standard. FMA, PSD2, GDPR. Via PSP auth. AT/EU. Bank account and transaction data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Austrian bank transfer. FMA/PSD2. Via PSP. AT/EU. Bank account data. Consumer online banking credentials never exposed to merchant.
